front: Which Part of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
From which country were Directive Principles of State Policy borrowed?
Are Directive Principles of State Policy justiciable in court?
back: No. Article 37 clarifies that DPSPs are non-justiciable.

front: Which Article directs the State to organize Village Panchayats?
Which Article directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code (UCC)?
Which Article mandates the separation of Judiciary from Executive?
Which Committee recommended adding Fundamental Duties to the Constitution?
Which Amendment Act added Part IVA and the first 10 Fundamental Duties?
Which Amendment Act added the 11th Fundamental Duty [Article 51A(k)]?
What age group is specified in the 11th Fundamental Duty regarding education?
back: Children between 6 and 14 years of age.
